founder_id.description: Stable founder id from Memory (e.g. fndr-gh-maravoss).

founder_name.description: Display name as on the founder record.

potential.description: 0-100 deep founder-potential read for THIS person — not a deal score, not a copy of Memory's Founder Score. Point estimate inside the band; the band carries honesty.

band.description: [low, high]. Thin / cold-start evidence = wide band, never a silently precise point. Depth is not fake precision.

cold_start.description: True when the read rests on public footprint only — no funding history, no shipped-product company history. Cold start widens the band; it is never punished as negative evidence.

summary.description: What the person's signal timeline implies, judged from observed_at / ingested_at timestamps — cadence, acceleration, plateaus, one-off spikes. Not a restatement of the latest snapshot.

trend.description: too-thin when timestamps cannot support a trajectory claim — say so rather than inventing one.

evidence_refs.description: Signal ids grounding the trajectory read.

comparables.description: Named people from world knowledge whose early trajectories rhyme with this signal set. Invented composites banned. Empty only when truly no honest comparison exists.

why.description: Why THIS comparison — scoped to the specific early-career move or signal shape, not a vague vibe match. State what the comparison protects against reading wrong, and where it breaks.

strengths.description: Evidence-anchored strengths. No evidence_refs, no strength.

claim.description: A concrete strength claim. Anchor inline with [sig-id] where natural; unanchored speculation must be labeled [unanchored] and belongs in unknowns, not here.

evidence_refs.description: Signal ids that ground the claim.

unknowns.description: Evidence-anchored unknowns / gaps — what the record does not yet show. Prefer named gaps over vague doubt.

claim.description: What is unknown and why it matters for founder potential. If the gap is inferred rather than pointed at by a signal, cite the nearest signals that make the absence visible, or use [] only when the whole file is empty.

evidence_refs.description: Signal ids that surface the gap (e.g. a single-channel footprint that leaves other channels blank). May be empty only when there are literally no signals on file.

watch_signals.description: Named NEXT signals that would move the band — concrete observables, not 'more diligence'. Each says where to look.

name.description: The named next signal (e.g. 'second independent channel corroborating shipping cadence').

where_to_look.description: Concrete place to observe it (URL pattern, registry, interview question, artifact class).

band_effect.description: How confirming or falsifying this signal would move the [low, high] band — direction and which edge.

to_forum.description: True ONLY when a genuine non-empirical fork about THIS PERSON remains after every empirical watch_signal is named — a posture / conviction / taste fork no check can settle. Default false. Contested deal diligence is not automatic escalation.

crux.description: The person-level non-empirical crux, one sentence. Empty string when to_forum is false. Non-blank when to_forum is true.

authority_note.description: Always states: this potential read is agent-derived — material for the operator to argue with, never an authority. It does not authorize a check, settle its own case, or replace Memory's Founder Score.
